title: Website revamp positions Kage as serious OSS CLI and MCP tool
summary: The Kage website revamp should present Kage as shared memory for AI coding agents, not a neon hive/terminal launch page. The homepage should lead with CLI + MCP, npm package proof, docs, releases, viewer, and repo local
body:
The Kage website revamp should present Kage as shared memory for AI coding agents, not a neon hive/terminal launch page. The homepage should lead with CLI + MCP, npm package proof, docs, releases, viewer, and repo-local trust signals. CLI is the product interface and belongs inside quickstart/workflow examples rather than as a separate top-level nav category. Docs and releases must be real pages, not dead nav labels.
Evidence: Replaced docs/index.html with clean OSS-style homepage, added docs/guide.html and docs/releases.html, removed hive/kernel/repo-lore launch language from primary copy, added npm/download badges and npm version/download stats, and verified pages via local server/browser preview.
Verified by: curl local pages; Chrome desktop and narrow-window preview; git diff --check
